'''Tibannopolis''' was the second largest city on [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]], after [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]]. It was one of Bespin's several [[Tibanna/Legends|tibanna gas]] [[Mining/Legends|mining]] operations.

==History==
[[File:Map_of_Tibannopolis.png|thumb|left|250px|The layout of Tibannopolis]]
The city was constructed in close [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]], and its builders hoped it would one day rival [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] in terms of both gas mining and popularity. Its inability to compete on these terms caused its citizens to gradually abandon it during the ferocious [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

Left to float aimlessly, the city was often plundered by inhabitants of other colonies whenever they found useful materials. What remained of the city sprawl became little more than a ghost town in the clouds. Over the years, its [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlifts]] continued to operate, but the failure of a [[Power regulator/Legends|power regulator]] caused the entire platform to list slightly to one side.<ref name="GORW">''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''</ref>

The [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] once met with [[Aurra Sing/Legends|Aurra Sing]] in order to get his [[Firespray-31-class patrol and attack craft/Legends|Firespray-31-class patrol and attack craft]], [[Slave I/Legends|Slave I]], back from Sing. Fett wanted the ship since it had belonged to his father, [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], and arrived in a [[Cloud car/Legends|cloud car]]. The cloud car came in handy when Sing broke the deal and flew off, and Fett managed to get aboard after chasing her down.<ref name="BF: Crossfire">''[[Boba Fett: Crossfire]]''</ref>

At one point, an [[IG-series]] assassin droid was hiding in the city. A [[Bespin Wing Guard/Legends|Bespin Wing Guard]] learned of this during a patrol and he had to track down the dangerous droid.<ref name="Hasbro">{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: Power of the Jedi|pack=Bespin Guard|altlink=https://galacticfigures.com/figureDetails.aspx?id=746}} {{C|Jedi Force File}}</ref>

After the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] occupation of Cloud City, Tibannopolis lay almost completely uninhabited. Strangely enough, it was one of a relatively few colonies left operational when its mining operation failed to gain a profit.

The [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Streen]] came to depend on Tibannopolis as a refuge, in between his prospecting of Bespin's gases, in the time frame around the famous [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].

==Behind the scenes==
In ''[[The Jedi Academy Sourcebook]]'', Tibannopolis is repeatedly spelled "Tibannoplis."
